name: Antônio Carlos Jobim
description: Brazilian songwriter, composer, arranger, singer, and pianist/guitarist (1927–1994)
abstract: Antônio Carlos Brasileiro de Almeida Jobim (25 January 1927 – 8 December 1994), also known as Tom Jobim (Portuguese pronunciation: [tõ ʒoˈbĩ] ), was a Brazilian composer, pianist, guitarist, songwriter, arranger and singer. Jobim is considered a great exponent of Brazilian music and one of the fathers of bossa nova for having merged samb…

about_quotes.speaker: Jon Hendricks
about_quotes.evidence: I met Jobim and all those people. I got very acquainted with them, well acquainted with them
about_quotes.doc_title: Jon Hendricks — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Allen
about_quotes.evidence: Another of the best really great composers of the recent decades is Antonio Carlos Jobim and some of his fellow South American composers. It was Jobim who brought back melody into American popular music when it had been pretty well cast aside.
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Allen interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Hubert Laws
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Next date is with Sergio Mendes, and Antonio Carlos Jobim is on it. [INTERVIEWEE] The Swinger from Rio.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hubert Laws — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Slide Hampton
about_quotes.evidence: I was listening to Jobim... we did an album of Jobim’s music... paying a tribute to him
about_quotes.doc_title: Slide Hampton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
